# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.

Prevent insecure deserialization.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

require 'json'
require 'base64'
require 'openssl'

module DistributedSession
  class SecureCookieStore
    HMAC_ALGORITHM = 'SHA256'

    def initialize(secret_key:, cookie_name: :session, max_age: 3600)
      raise ArgumentError, "secret_key must be at least 32 bytes" if secret_key.nil? || secret_key.bytesize < 32

      @secret_key = secret_key
      @cookie_name = cookie_name
      @max_age = max_age
    end

    # Serialize session data securely using JSON (safe serialization format)
    # and sign it with HMAC to prevent tampering.
    def serialize(session_data)
      payload = {
        data: session_data,
        exp: Time.now.to_i + @max_age
      }

      # Use JSON instead of Marshal — JSON cannot deserialize into arbitrary objects
      json_payload = JSON.generate(payload)
      encoded_payload = Base64.strict_encode64(json_payload)
      signature = generate_hmac(encoded_payload)

      "#{encoded_payload}--#{signature}"
    end

    # Deserialize session data securely.
    # Verifies HMAC signature before deserializing to prevent tampering.
    # Uses JSON.parse (safe) instead of Marshal.load (unsafe).
    def deserialize(signed_cookie_value)
      return {} if signed_cookie_value.nil? || signed_cookie_value.empty?

      encoded_payload, signature = signed_cookie_value.split('--', 2)

      return {} if encoded_payload.nil? || signature.nil?

      # Verify HMAC signature using constant-time comparison
      expected_signature = generate_hmac(encoded_payload)
      unless secure_compare(expected_signature, signature)
        Rails.logger.warn("Session cookie signature verification failed — possible tampering attempt")
        return {}
      end

      json_payload = Base64.strict_decode64(encoded_payload)
      payload = JSON.parse(json_payload, symbolize_names: false)

      # Check expiration
      if payload['exp'].nil? || payload['exp'] < Time.now.to_i
        Rails.logger.info("Session cookie expired")
        return {}
      end

      payload['data'] || {}
    rescue ArgumentError, JSON::ParserError => e
      Rails.logger.warn("Failed to deserialize session cookie: #{e.message}")
      {}
    end

    # Write session to cookies
    def write(cookies, session_data)
      cookies[@cookie_name] = {
        value: serialize(session_data),
        httponly: true,
        secure: Rails.env.production?,
        same_site: :lax,
        expires: @max_age.seconds.from_now
      }
    end

    # Read session from cookies
    def read(cookies)
      deserialize(cookies[@cookie_name])
    end

    # Destroy session
    def destroy(cookies)
      cookies.delete(@cookie_name)
    end

    private

    def generate_hmac(data)
      OpenSSL::HMAC.hexdigest(HMAC_ALGORITHM, @secret_key, data)
    end

    # Constant-time string comparison to prevent timing attacks
    def secure_compare(a, b)
      return false unless a.bytesize == b.bytesize

      OpenSSL.fixed_length_secure_compare(a, b)
    end
  end
end

# Rails middleware integration
module DistributedSession
  class Middleware
    def initialize(app, secret_key:, cookie_name: :session)
      @app = app
      @store = SecureCookieStore.new(secret_key: secret_key, cookie_name: cookie_name)
    end

    def call(env)
      request = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
      env['distributed_session'] = @store.read(request.cookie_jar)

      status, headers, body = @app.call(env)

      response = Rack::Response.new(body, status, headers)
      if env['distributed_session.modified']
        @store.write(response, env['distributed_session'])
      end

      response.finish
    end
  end
end

# Example Rails concern for controllers
module DistributedSession
  module ControllerConcern
    extend ActiveSupport::Concern

    private

    def distributed_session
      request.env['distributed_session'] ||= {}
    end

    def distributed_session_set(key, value)
      distributed_session[key.to_s] = value
      request.env['distributed_session.modified'] = true
    end

    def distributed_session_get(key)
      distributed_session[key.to_s]
    end

    def destroy_distributed_session
      request.env['distributed_session'] = {}
      request.env['distributed_session.modified'] = true
    end
  end
end
